textByHashes.dc9ab249581ba0e62434d3d1ad353c5dbf6a284a: Ah, an adventurer. Could you spare some of your time to help me?
textByHashes.bdb05921e5421ee38373fde544c8ae8870d52a72: It's a sorry tale. I was once an accomplished sculptor, you see.
textByHashes.72cb599c2ea7d21343fbef8da54e4a947fc3d673: I made statues for the kings, queens and rulers of Gielinor. But then there was the accident...
textByHashes.e69858d0946df628a8898ceb02be73f0947759b3: After that, I could never hold a chisel again.
textByHashes.99cd635dc713989da7c50ffd882d26b0dcb87359: But I have been struck by my muse while travelling the desert, and want nothing more than to build a statue in this area.
textByHashes.0ce1dbf1bcb944ba1c139830701c6d0cef02a7b6: The desert's plain and full of sand, and needs more decoration.
textByHashes.59a6d8af9cd01a103d5688dff7c2b851ca8ecbdb: As I said, I can't hold a chisel, but I can guide you in crafting the statue for me.
textByHashes.390545c9f58028010fe67b31eb9f9f424cf85b9b: I'd need you to quarry the stone for it, as well.
textByHashes.c5ad5caf5d20c658e58355b7577d6cba36779f66: Well, will you help me with my art?
textByHashes.876602b09e4a69aaeef842cbd4cff686ada63877: There is a stone quarry nearby; I require several blocks of sandstone in order to make the statue.
textByHashes.e0e59be27c75c8d70916da0a2cf534e77a82a960: Each of these blocks should be around... well, let's say thirty two kilograms for the first one.
textByHashes.515cb4ad42ded5784b895edecf002e564c515dc5: Well, then, bring me enough smaller blocks to make thirty two kilograms, and I will deal with it.
textByHashes.978897dff17c20a8f7995370f81f55e0566c4eec: From what the last adventurer told me, there are tiny blocks that weigh 1 kg, small blocks that weigh 2 kg, and medium-sized blocks that weigh 5 kg.
textByHashes.dc2fb762f6aa79d3b9635a4930c9d02fc5f0fb43: There are also large blocks that weigh 10 kg, but I doubt a weedy adventurer like you could even carry one, let alone mine one cleanly.
textByHashes.e65dca0c4c02cc9461ef317db567e5bd6e98e83e: Apparently, the more skilled you are at mining, the larger the blocks you can chip off the rock.
textByHashes.1d994c2dec4480edbb63edfb6ab854ab3fdd29f2: That makes kg I'm carrying now.
textByHashes.7b884d4c790543e8e7441da66a8998d9f400340d: I'm now carrying kg of your inexpertly-quarried stone.
textByHashes.e643bf52e898d3e7524283cc16c453c67e55f051: I need kg more stone to make the base. Any more stone you want to burden me down with?
textByHashes.8a0868d5edbf0570cc5132f9fb890281ff324632: I need kg more stone to make the base. Have you any more to make me carry?
textByHashes.eefaf9018129f7ead02daaff51b33732693deadc: I need kg more stone to make the base. Do you have any more stone you want to offload onto me?
textByHashes.02e0138eaf38c8af0e3d871501a3c64f5c52de5b: Oh, joy. I'm sure I'll treasure it.
textByHashes.6eb2e45ff6a6232e831994a5d97bb398bd5e7669: Either you've been splitting stone into smaller blocks, or you're worse at quarrying stone than I expected.
textByHashes.8add337f0e7dd4f8f28233d5438e39ca7bbc5f33: Because, adventurer, I'm not going to carry more of your blocks than I absolutely must.
textByHashes.81b5c3d9f9d3d2a6e29e9ae8667171b4d609940e: If your blocks are too big, break them with a chisel or a pick.
textByHashes.e0953799747d3760ed68c180e35049efb1424dca: That's enough for the base of the statue, certainly.
textByHashes.30dd4232efa06aeb6ad892ca7d5ac1cb0eb83792: Here you are, adventurer. Now, use a chisel to craft it into a base for the statue.
textByHashes.0d4a71dfd33bcf1f26b9431697e5c0ce088984a7: Oh, stop whining. The last adventurer who helped me was carrying around a girl in a barrel.
textByHashes.c67ef6391c3655058716772647691170ab3431de: Zamorak only knows why, but he didn't complain.
textByHashes.d5f1d97a453c0a206586b6b6762e49a255d755ea: Zamorak only knows why, but she didn't complain.
textByHashes.f4f666edf01db3ae9aad96115ed7eb63ad988455: I told you to carve it into the base of the statue, correct?
textByHashes.fa12d7d970678f244b84b76ee3558cc330c34928: Then why are you standing around brainlessly? Get on with it!
textByHashes.7550abc860dd5af1c29947c2f6b851b1a68c684f: Excellent. Now, just put it on the flat ground next to me.
textByHashes.7901c94e02537e99a247d0d8c06e34864528ce73: Hmph. Well, the base of the statue is done, but it needs a body. Twenty kilograms should be enough for you to make it.
textByHashes.01f61ef0e0b86c4876e83c1fee00a27cacee1fac: That makes kg I'm carrying now.
textByHashes.768d2ffc6be211735dedb49db302d438ad22b36c: I'm now carrying kg of your inexpertly-quarried stone.
textByHashes.3d65d2de222755e5ffe47fb63f5b88be5fd7cd3d: I need kg more stone to make the body. Any more stone you want to burden me down with?
textByHashes.d9590043e15b607babdc9dd326764d4b87c09935: I need kg more stone to make the body. Have you any more to make me carry?
textByHashes.129b93913f432973bbf5669e43bfe866d0ed2188: I need kg more stone to make the body. Do you have any more stone you want to offload onto me?
textByHashes.51df3b9c9272eaeeae4572cf97bcff747caeaa84: That should be enough to use for the statue's body.
textByHashes.fe6b86144d4e4e9b8d5eb79456522697673cb2bd: All you have to do with it, adventurer, is chisel it into the body of the statue.
textByHashes.9fa7e6a55832b353abac1efa8ed21e43b5602156: Very nice, adventurer. You're supposed to make it into the statue's body.
textByHashes.acb907eec05998589337cda3bab940bfdb7adb88: Get your chisel ready and start working.
textByHashes.3101c0f10561f68363467aba917c7879efd47a74: Well done, adventurer. Not as dense as you look.
textByHashes.800a9684402424c7b66fac32a2ab3b43251aaca7: Go on, add it to the base of the statue.
textByHashes.ef69fd62501199a5224b71fcbcdfa49c15881817: You utter incompetent! Have you added the body to the statue yet?
textByHashes.41ef4ce7f5e35f0536ccaac9166a4112e071ecc8: Hmm, then perhaps I'm still missing something...
textByHashes.c7d8c08630ab6fba09aac2c61b1a23f5e5b44d4b: Oh, I don't know, adventurer. Go and chisel the statue a bit more, or something.
textByHashes.930d258b02f09c98bd001ec7723a21bedaf85d92: It shouldn't have taken this long... did I miscalculate?
textByHashes.c725c45be7f61f8c6ad58ed1c54a9c5d2f818668: Ah, adventurer. As you can see, the statue is nearly complete. All it needs is a head.
textByHashes.c3e7ac3cd89c20f60a5488ac5b18aac932530ca2: The head should be made out of granite, which you can find in that quarry nearby.
textByHashes.f7fd889131f16d3a3b5ddc5ba64bd37334c09f63: As for whose head... well, perhaps I'll let you impose your own artistic vision on it.
textByHashes.3a38bdd991eacf23470b45901e41ee67e2f4afb0: Whose head do you think should be on the statue?
textByHashes.0949c232b461f9151ec3c5e4085aadfe7863d4ca: You'd start carving it, then you'd take a helmet off or change your hairstyle, and nobody would know who on Gielinor it was supposed to be!
textByHashes.24d866171e7f5a419473e5ef4a959e977c29255e: Statues are far more permanent than you adventurers and your fashions.
textByHashes.9b336af85e052b5a7e4453169c05dbc9dcdd5d03: Why, that's very generous of you, adventurer.
textByHashes.fd746c0585b3ef041c727a77004f6734d6842ced: Are you hoping for a bigger reward for saying that?
textByHashes.e58d0d6bc9007311be6f80da67b6a29c5b8eeb0e: Wonderful. Well, at least you have me here for reference.
textByHashes.9753d6e36fd5c83c9e819d6773fcf1afda1fc3f5: Now, go and find a suitable block of granite and make that head.
textByHashes.92fbe99d2be0abe320b3b9db64a5b7d88e234838: And do try to remember which one it is you're carving.
textByHashes.4037dfc5762ab603897b77efb1906fdd091c2674: And which god were you thinking of? There's very little reference material here in the desert.
textByHashes.7ebda06494155d45e29f4dd7586dd0ffd661e229: Well, some of the people in the desert worship a god called Icthlarin.
textByHashes.3bd7427a265ca4fb58305ea449e0169d45a1bbb1: Or, if you prefer, I'm sure you could sculpt Zamorak instead.
textByHashes.e7a75ede1e12572816120ba1bd283313d024ec21: Now you should find a suitable block of granite, and make that head.
textByHashes.f30ed3ffa85c85949fa68e141691ef8517267748: Try to remember which head you've decided to carve - I know you adventurers. Memories like fishing nets.
textByHashes.8298822a945116755960211ea8174b28371f82ac: An interesting choice - I'm sure there's some reference material you can use around here.
textByHashes.935b05bd8f58e28702c1d6f5b19b98a18485d83f: Find a suitable block of granite, and get carving.
textByHashes.20173068686b0ad4a7063c8b0619595620bac959: And make sure to remember which one you're supposed to be carving. I've known some of you adventurers to forget things you were told three seconds ago.
textByHashes.a6510583c8e6438798bca560102da9e85be9c694: A human statue with a camel's head? Is there any particular reason?
textByHashes.41f2ee38ddccb09db1dff09ec044c5c0764549cd: Well, it is appropriate, all things considered...
textByHashes.228466c8ddebf19c1294f27606db7cc510d053f8: Very well, it'll work well in a desert setting. Now, find a suitable block of granite, and carve that head.
textByHashes.0c67149db14137f4f8891122b593db44b30dc95f: Make sure you remember which head you're carving. The last adventurer who tried to help me forgot half- way through carving, and produced the strangest mixture of camel and human I've ever seen.
textByHashes.1732219aa618dfeb15a27d34c6e3ef17df388890: Keldagrim? Hmph. Technically good sculptors, the dwarves, but they have no sense of art.
textByHashes.20dd3d27d5700811462ff4f31a1b6aebbdd0290e: I'm sure that none of them would even think of travelling to the desert and building a statue here.
textByHashes.fd128349aba22c8a423a27d5487979bcd87c2b7f: Do you even remember what it looked like?
textByHashes.20470b81eb87e74dd91d748f3363fd4adb8e1ee5: Then no, you can't. Now, choose something else.
textByHashes.a1af49ddb2b5354154c02d3b5fdc3440a554f347: You decided to make the statue's head look like me, you said.
textByHashes.736c39bd944efff8814355e7003840c39a70136a: Hmm. I don't think much of your artistic skills, adventurer, but it's a passable likeness.
textByHashes.1e3d9e2f74c7a6692174cac4e31ee8e7cc63db0e: ...You really have a shorter attention span than a grain of sand, don't you?
textByHashes.e475675cbde30c2861e3b5f1445541673861bcc3: You said you'd make the statue's head look like me, not like modern art!
textByHashes.dba00f7b55b4505d9c74deb16f9c1f55c64106a8: Perhaps it was too difficult for you. Do you want to change what head you'll make?
textByHashes.fd170399b4c08f70afa1a00c916b2c11fbe99064: Fair enough. Remember, you're carving my head for the statue.
textByHashes.774ff6567f2c858879a83a1adf83c40e56009f09: Well, which will you choose this time?
textByHashes.0a28086eb305a161f0e71ad27c36337110b6c65a: You said you'd make the statue's head look like Zamorak.
textByHashes.d75744352fa9d6fcd18d1ea55e95f305e8dd5cca: Hmm. You're not a particularly good sculptor, adventurer, but it's at least recognisable.
textByHashes.b21a47d60943f7068a68dcebbd2acb3676fee785: Do you even remember who you were supposed be carving?
textByHashes.a352fb426f963f03d476d40100ca80461422a1e8: It was supposed to be Zamorak, not the horrific monstrosity you've carved!
textByHashes.4e957655854bef24d120164068e52bfdd5e480b3: Ahem, that is to say, er...
textByHashes.98368dea1bbcba1cfef677e807f7c3d1be9177a0: Well, it was obviously too tough for you. Do you want to change what head you'll make?
textByHashes.a4d5646397405bb873adbea045cc1daad4de2b0a: Well, you said that you'd make the statue's head look like Icthlarin.
textByHashes.f2487b1e3c82a99c290ff4e5e099cfda6a977607: You're not much of an artist, but at least you did your research. It'll pass.
textByHashes.09412277e484705848b3c758dbc4d0ad42d9c39e: Weren't you supposed to be carving Icthlarin's head?
textByHashes.9cef565d38988079e1033678f145452ec6b7854e: Well, you're paying lots of attention, aren't you? Honestly, you adventurers.
textByHashes.7df825e0125aad67e9f61f3887f4de21265339c9: It would be easier on your poor little brain if you changed what head you'll make, don't you agree?
textByHashes.3feebdaab07780e724ddfc2570803a26c2e4beb7: You said you'd make the statue's head look like a camel, and that looks like...
textByHashes.059f084cb8824c926d042b4091b765f8627ab0e6: Well, it wouldn't fool a camel, but it's close enough for the statue.
textByHashes.d515ff8c63b9873461b2e5b72568dae778927979: I don't believe it. You said you were planning to make a camel head, but what's this?
textByHashes.281e6fc9d540c54d13c78f13ec0e3a0cc722dc86: Of course not! Why would I give something to you for having a worse memory than a piece of burnt fish?
textByHashes.d38936962b98eff7da897abe26626ce82d2b4c8d: Do you want to change what head you'll make to something you have a chance of remembering?
textByHashes.9a609bc5d115cdcb02152dfb5c84dbd5fd4de76f: Hold it! I know you adventurers.
textByHashes.6be2ccb114df1451481fc62946a9b296c8cd631c: I bet you've managed to make the wrong head, or something like that.
textByHashes.76a92db2dd6f4e31160c25f6bd0d2aede8029104: I don't care if you think you know what you're doing. Let me have a look at that head.
textByHashes.57f8abe125482533e74e5d78d990d0606a969217: Ah, excellent, it's all gone as planned.
textByHashes.fc439ae90be637ed7e96cfd5cc0a6905be678b79: Of course we did! That's why I had you build a statue.
textByHashes.144bffc0cd4685f15ffe519cfeff21c88a5f0bda: Oh, nothing. No treasure here. No priceless artefacts from the lost ages of Gielinor, or anything like that.
textByHashes.9f6569dcc2bcc8e27981f374851a6d98edd77fb8: Wait! If you help me, then...
textByHashes.90c91643e232de7c965050743954c8506651d2fe: I'll give you a share of whatever we find.
textByHashes.10adbfcdfbc812624094ed687eea3699b1d0272f: At the top of the temple. We've fallen through to the very bottom, so you'll have to climb up to the next floor.
textByHashes.6c035c658583753186e5175ca55a54d474415718: The stories I've heard say there are odd doors in this temple, that have locks that don't need keys, but... rather large limbs.
textByHashes.1b2bfff35e3446704643e9be90d0cdbc5778a9b5: You can use your chisel or pick to take them from the statue. They should be the right size.
textByHashes.5412f68e3e2000ab25ec0115181cced9e438c0c3: Be thankful they're the statue's limbs and not yours, adventurer.
textByHashes.2a7e51dceb10d8302789f16ee1e951cd8e378759: Ah, it fell off when the statue dropped through the ceiling. I've been keeping it safe since then.
textByHashes.ac57d1c909a8962456e8ddf8fba3a525d0f6d8ca: Try not to lose it, adventurer.
textByHashes.9c4669407cb1993abde5a9c3cdb0185e97cd5044: You didn't get far from here before you mislaid it.
textByHashes.ede5fefd0f7dea2d9901614d107e7ebbd77204c0: This time, try not to drop it.
textByHashes.1803ed0fe5b7e09fbf66e122e83ee06d33d9289f: Been at the Asgarnian Ale a little too much, have we?
textByHashes.bc79cf89b07a94e360804c7d77b1372b33b62520: Of course not! I'm not a carnival conjurer.
textByHashes.14784934af5cabd1e868c911d305d4b1d45e43eb: Adversity builds character, adventurer. Heft them around by yourself.
textByHashes.be8c7862d6eb5b57be6aa1d2d51ccfc9ec988177: I suppose I'm useful to have around at these times. I found it when you dropped it around here.
textByHashes.cb06a2f3ad2b6d6520b8a4e01be8fb2b6aa18d90: It's lucky for you that I notice these things. Here, you managed to drop it in this room.
textByHashes.6409c273c69616b45f87f1c72f2d669a3f56c2a4: You dropped it near the statue. Do I have to hold your hand through everything?
textByHashes.ebd8407366be8efe1f6082aa11ec5f2bce84ea2a: Well, it didn't go far - I found it in here. Take better care of it next time.
textByHashes.e19477edfa6cf3964ee680d76c46c50f947f05b0: Really? Did it have some sort of recess on the top?
textByHashes.8becf68cfbd1498df02ead295a1472f60df7b035: In that case, perhaps you should try using the head of the statue on it.
textByHashes.a0de48510a7bc5343b32f3a64e5f09ef3ac95d47: Perhaps you should find out what shape the recess is, so you can sculpt something that will fit in it.
textByHashes.93f28258486a37dc64afb4b2f0c77f12f6d731e6: Adventurer, I know more about this temple than you do.
textByHashes.1b5a9c962fcbe5a15c937e3e8f5415f38d397bad: All you need to do is make a mould of the recess somehow, then use it as a guide to make another head.
textByHashes.599f8dbc1e61cbf9938241f0f9c2f6a74d40719d: It should be made from the same granite as the statue's head was.
textByHashes.5a093bff969f3af9297cb4ae63587ba977739ce3: Well done unlocking those doors, adventurer. Did you have any problems?
textByHashes.8fe57e1db798e7f55e8bf7202f677d126a3ab7ad: Those are some of the traps to keep people away from this temple.
textByHashes.81f87c93d2a9caa957b47e9a80a634a262b5726c: Thankfully, they didn't send you mad this time.
textByHashes.bd40c9c7a2c43985e60dfb2600b654301c7f5283: No, there is another floor above this. You have to go up a ladder in the room to the north.
textByHashes.af8787ec24f0392cf1eacd8c51d09acb83a70f94: There's a magic barrier in the way. And right now, it won't let anything through.
textByHashes.9d24f00484d3fce38286286f543a1cadd6a71d2c: No, but I suspect the other rooms on this floor have magical properties. If you solve all the puzzles they present, then...
textByHashes.bd58fca7d41e726f732be6879b0929d44680785f: The globes on the pedestal here will also light when you've solved the room they correspond to.
textByHashes.fc7fac8208ba5e704c4c002682f2f60a97a916d1: The treasure's up ahead, adventurer. Be careful of the traps.
textByHashes.cd87038ddf2a44398331ddcaf24be5c66909af3f: Oh, I don't know. Perhaps, say, huge skeletons that'll tear your arms and legs off?
textByHashes.5509d72f1b6e235c2a5473391af4b953be899002: Nothing, adventurer, nothing. You just trot along and get that treasure, now.
textByHashes.50001874040c31405e22df314c13d6983dca1457: Well, adventurer, do you have the treasure yet?
textByHashes.00dc202ac60e2b41eed6715b7a6ff349572bd000: Ah ha! So, it's not attacking you? Well done, adventurer. Your second brain cell must be working overtime today.
textByHashes.5502691061c5d14b95cec0df0a506182ca422e28: Well, thank you, adventurer. You've just deprived me of a wonderful chance to learn from one of Zamorak's first followers.
textByHashes.449335bb68c6b3ae651ff49dcec8268e2a31e1b2: You utter imbecile! Did you think I was looking for gold?
textByHashes.11f554d5716748c9ffd1f31d18de0e0f332e36bd: [sigh] I might as well stay to study this temple. It has some interesting magical properties.
textByHashes.94fa434c65c9221525af9ad72285920ed82fcfcf: I'm sure I've dropped hints the size of Varrock, but you might not have noticed. I'm a Zamorakian mage.
textByHashes.cb7876c9e7b111a80b4021a9e3b4a3a5ff191a2a: If you really want to learn my spells, you'll have to dedicate yourself to Zamorak.
textByHashes.2968bb4e80c876607647a8b9124e7304056a2767: Then you won't be learning any of my spells.
textByHashes.f4b8ea45f17a8cd51bdb0dde38d1f027dd805cc6: What did you expect from a Zamorakian mage?
textByHashes.74a8b4e8bd5ef368ac438b39bb69f1c913df6505: You really thought I would teach you my secrets just like that? Ha ha ha! You are a naive fool.
textByHashes.c836b29abc33307cdbc9b8c5bd5161f20a736b67: I'm a mage of Zamorak, after all. What else do you expect?
textByHashes.b59686f37a304e48d63f38a3d517fd2a7b708141: How are you and your defective brain doing nowadays, adventurer?
textByHashes.0723391b5b2970c2b9076b03c5249146f6f20bea: You scatterbrained fool. You dropped it in here. Since you have no space to carry it, I can't give it back yet.
textByHashes.912b138fec29b7746d58316c7774f3d04fa41459: You really are an idiot, aren't you? You dropped it in here when you left. Try not to lose it next time.
textByHashes.911885483b98c7a02850bf4abb9cc0e235d4cb96: I'm not very nice. If you want to see who ruined all of my plans, adventurer, just look in a mirror.
textByHashes.23d1c1948f6fdae75e984816c3b24b8e86fb5d18: And don't forget who gave it unlimited charges for you!
textByHashes.9f0125f6ae304ccac71d82595e03aa03ee625abe: About that amulet - I might be able to improve it for you, for a fee. Have you got it with you?
textByHashes.33be7731c3e9573ffdd0e4d83937ce44597b71b5: Of course you did. I bet you spend your life collecting junk, storing it, then complaining you've run out of storage space. Fetch the amulet if you want my help.
textByHashes.b11e00615980a310e6422691613d666f8e7d0252: Yes, it's what I thought. This amulet will run out of charges all the time, if you keep using it. For one million coins I can give it unlimited charges.
textByHashes.540ec6c12ae2c20f8e28b8d5bdbfb1807228e4c0: Three reasons. Firstly, I'm a Zamorakian mage - we're not big on charity work. Secondly, I want the money. Finally, in case you need reminding, I hate you.
